rulings.Case Title: BARRY G. INGRAM VS. STONESTOWN GALLERIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, May 1, 2014, Line 6: DEFENDANTS JAMES ADAMS, SAMSON SABA, RICK FORSTER, and STONESTOWN SHOPPING CENTER, L.P.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication. The Court grants defendant's motion for summary judgment. The declaration of Samson Saba establishes that defendant's conduct was not motivated by race. Defendant has maintained its initial burden of production. In opposition, plaintiff fails to present competent evidence demonstrating a triable issue of material fact regarding that issue. Even if Mr. Saba was mistaken regarding the type of wheels on the cart, plaintiff fails to show proof of discriminatory animus. The Court further notes that plaintiff failed to provide an opposing separate statement and, since defendant has met its initial burden of production, the failure to provide an opposing separate statement is a separate and alternative ground to grant defendants' motion under CCP 437c(b)(3). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: BARRY G. INGRAM VS. STONESTOWN GALLERIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, May 1, 2014, Line 6: PLAINTIFF BARRY INGRAM'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The Court denies Plaintiff's motion for summary judgment. Plaintiff has not met his initial burden of production of establishing that there is no triable issue of material fact. Further, Plaintiff's motion is not supported by competent evidence. The evidence is not supported by a declaration. Defendants' objections to evidence are sustained. As a separate and alternative ground for denying the motion, Plaintiff has failed to provide a separate statement as required by CCP 437c(b)(1). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: CAPITAL ONE BANK (USA), N.A. VS. BINH HOANG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, May 1, 2014, Line 7: DEFENDANT BINH HOANG'S MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT. The motion is denied. The mandatory relief provisions of CCP 473 do not apply to defendant because defendant is seeking relief from an order granting summary judgment and judgment entered thereon. See English v. IKON Business Solutions, Inc., (2001) 94 Cal. App. 4th 130, 138; Prieto v. Loyola Marymount Univ. (2005) 132 Cal.App.4th 290, 297; Las Vegas Land and Development Company, LLC v. Wilkie Way, LLC (2013) 219 Cal.App.4th 1086. "A summary judgment is neither a 'default,' nor a 'default judgment,' nor a 'dismissal' within the meaning of section 473(b)." English v. IKON Business Solutions, Inc., supra, 94 Cal. App. 4th at 133. Defendant's motion appears to be based solely on the mandatory provisions of CCP 473. In the last sentence of its reply brief, Defendant states that even if the mandatory provision is not applicable, "discretionary relief is still available to Defendant." Reply Br. at 3. However, Defendant fails to establish any factual basis for relief under the discretionary portions of CCP 473. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: BONNIE LOW VS. KWONG YIP CHEUNG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, May 01, 2014, Line 2, Plaintiff'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories Pro Tem Judge James Watson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is denied, without prejudice to the service of discovery requests which are specifically targeted to matters genuinely at issue in the case. The ominibus disovery demands are not reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of relevant and admissible evidence. The discovery requests are overbroad. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to jamesw@skwsf.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JENNY FARABEE VS. HEATHER MICHELLE GARRITY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, May 1, 2014, Line 16: DEFENDANTS HEATHER GARRITY, ETHAN GARRITY, and DOMINIC GARRITY'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. The anti-SLAPP motion to strike is denied. The allegations of the amended complaint do not implicate a public issue under CCP 425.16(e)(4). See Rivero v. American Federation of State, County and Municipal Employees, AFL-CIO (2003) 105 Cal.App.4th 913, 925. Defendant fails to meet its burden on the first prong of the anti-SLAPP statute. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: JENNY FARABEE VS. HEATHER MICHELLE GARRITY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, May 1, 2014, Line 16: DEFENDANTS HEATHER GARRITY, ETHAN GARRITY, and DOMINIC GARRITY'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. The demurrer to the amended complaint is overruled in its entirety. The complaint alleges sufficient facts to support the stalking cause of action. (See par. 22 of the amended complaint). The complaint alleges sufficient facts to support the intrusion cause of action. (See par. 21 of the amended complaint). The complaint alleges sufficient facts to support the false light cause of action and the claim stands or falls with the defamation claim. The complaint alleges sufficient facts to support the harassment cause of action. (See pars. 17 and 18 of the amended complaint). The complaint alleges sufficient facts to support the defamation cause of action and it is not time barred. (See par. 19 of the amended complaint). The complaint alleges sufficient facts to support the intentional infliction of emotional distress cause of action. (See pars. 58 and 59 of the amended complaint). The complaint alleges sufficient facts to support the interference cause of action. (See pars. 61-65 of the amended complaint). The complaint alleges sufficient facts to support the negligent supervision cause of action. (See pars. 66-70 of the amended complaint). The Court agrees that conspiracy is not a distinct cause of action. The Court will treat the "ninth cause of action" for conspiracy as general charging allegations. The conspiracy theory is viable because plaintiff pleads an underlying wrong and an agreement. To attack the recovery of damages, defendants should have filed a motion to strike. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)
